input.headline: How do you judge the world’s best cheese?

raw.headline: How do you judge the world’s best cheese?

winner: Why don't we send a blindfolded mouse to international cheese competitions? Because it already knows the world's best cheese by taste!

candidates.text: Why don't we send a blindfolded mouse to international cheese competitions? Because it already knows the world's best cheese by taste!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist with a clever play on taste and mice, concise and specific."}

candidates.text: Why don't we let cows decide who makes the world's best cheese? Because they'd just moo-ve it to pasteurization!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.0, "reason": "Puns on 'moo-ve' and 'pasteurization' are clever and specific, with a good surprise factor and natural flow."}

candidates.text: Why don't cheese experts ever get lost? Because they always follow their gouda instincts!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 7.5, "reason": "The joke is clever with a play on 'gouda' and 'good' instincts, but the surprise and specificity could be stronger."}

candidates.text: Why do cows compete for the title of the world's best cheese maker? Because they've always thought moo-ving cheese was beneath them!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat clever with the pun on 'moo-ving,' but it doesn't directly address the headline about judging cheese. It's a bit generic and lacks specificity in connecting cows to cheese judging."}

candidates.text: Apparently, judging the world's best cheese isn't as easy as just eating it with a side of crackers.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat surprising but lacks specificity and punch. It's a mild play on expectations but doesn't fully deliver on the humor potential of the headline."}
